article_title: Updates to liquor licensing process for private corporations

briefs.title: BC liquor licence rules now include directors and officers in tied house checks
briefs.summary: BC's Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch (LCRB) has updated its liquor licensing rules for private corporations. Directors and officers are now counted when deciding if a 'tied house' relationship exists — meaning a business connection that could pressure a licensee to favour one supplier's alcohol over others. A new Legal Entity Review process also lets the LCRB formally request corporate documents through the online licensing portal.
briefs.action_required: Log in to the BC liquor and cannabis licensing portal to report any tied house associations under your licence.
briefs.article_title: Updates to liquor licensing process for private corporations
